trails.name: Boundary stream walkway
trails.description: Boundary Stream Walkway is 60km north of Napier. It can be accessed from Pohokura Road, at its western end, or Heays Access Road, at its eastern end.

Cameron carpark tramping tracks
trails.description: From the Cameron carpark you can walk to Kuripapaango Trig or Cameron Hut. Tramps to Kiwi Saddle, Kaweka Range, Kiwi Mouth Hut and Back Ridge are also available.

trails.name: Wakarara Road end routes
trails.description: There are a variety of tramping routes from Wakarara road end in the Eastern Ruahine Forest Park, including Gold Creek and Barlow Hut.

trails.name: A’Deanes Bush Walk
trails.description: Take a walk through A’Deanes Bush Scenic Reserve in beautiful native forest dominated by mataī  and kahikatea as well as a large tōtara tree. The reserve is on a flat river terrace.

trails.name: Ahuriri Estuary Walking Track
trails.description: View a community of wading birds, fish, mud dwellers and aquatic plant life from the estuary track and boardwalk. Over 70 species of resident and migratory waterbirds use the estuary as a feeding and resting area.

trails.name: Alice Nash Memorial Heritage Lodge Track
trails.description: This track in the scenic Oroua River valley can be enjoyed as a half day tramp but it's also a good overnight tramp for families with children and new trampers.

trails.name: Boundary Stream walks
trails.description: Walk to the Hawke's Bay highest waterfall or choose another walk in an area where native forest and birds are protected from pests and predators - shorter options are suitable for children

trails.name: Comet Road end to Shutes Hut
trails.description: From Comet Road End take a 2-3 hour walk to historic Shutes Hut, built in 1920 for rabbiters and musterers to use.

trails.name: Deerford Track to Makiekie Creek
trails.description: Walk to picturesque Makiekie Creek for a picnic and a paddle.

trails.name: Iron Gate Hut Track
trails.description: Iron Gate Hut is a good day's hike along a sidle track up the Oroua River valley. Longer tramps are possible from this hut. Stay a night and return the same way.

trails.name: Kairakau Loop Track
trails.description:
A great opportunity to walk through mature lowland
podocarp forest with excellent examples of kahikatea,
matai, miro and tōtara.

trails.name: Kashmir Road tramping tracks
trails.description: Kashmir Road climbs far and high into Ruahine Forest Park and provides quick access to the high country. Daphne Hut, Awatere Hut and Longview Hut are all accessible by a track from the road.

trails.name: Kaweka Road tramping tracks
trails.description: From Kaweka Road you can access Makahu Saddle via Whittle Road. The saddle is a popular starting point for backcountry tramping trips.

trails.name: Kaweka Road walks
trails.description: From Kaweka Road you can access Makahu Saddle via Whittle Road. The saddle is a popular starting point for walks into the mountain tops.

trails.name: Kelly Knight Hut Track
trails.description: The track to Kelly Knight Hut crosses farmland to the Ruahine Forest Park boundary, then sidles above the Pourangaki River to reach the hut via a swingbridge. Stay a night and return the same way.

trails.name: Knights Track to Pohangina Valley East Road
trails.description: There is a bit of everything on this tramp in the Ruahine Forest Park - open tops, rivers and bush travel.

trails.name: Knights-Shorts Loop Track
trails.description: Take a strenuous day hike and enjoy the spectacular scenery from the tops of the Ngamoko Range in the Ruahine Forest Park.

trails.name: Kuripapango tramping tracks
trails.description: There are a variety of options for day or overnight trips from Kuripapango/Castle Rocks Road. Some are round trips and some link with other road ends or access points to Kaweka Forest Park.

trails.name: Lawrence Road tramping tracks
trails.description: From Lawrence Road take a 2-3 hour walk through Blowhard Bush or to Lotkow Road. From their follow the Lotkow Track for another 2-3 hours to Lookout and Donald River.

trails.name: Lotkow Road to Little's Clearing
trails.description: Lotkow Road branches off Whittle Road at the entry point to the Kaweka Forest Park.

trails.name: Makahu Road tramping tracks
trails.description: The narrow Makahu Road provides access to the Makino and Middle Hill huts.

trails.name: Mangleton Road to Park's Peak Ridge
trails.description: It’s a 2-3 hour walk from Mangleton Road to Park’s Peak Ridge. The track links to Ruahine Range and Golden Crown Ridge to the north and Park's Peak and Upper Makaroro huts to the south.

trails.name: Masters Shelter to Golden Crown Ridge
trails.description: The Golden Crown Ridge Track begins behind Masters Shelter. The 2-3 hour walk climbs around a series of rocky knolls before continuing steeply upwards through beech forest to the top of the range.

trails.name: McKinnon Hut Track
trails.description: Perched on the northern end of the Hikurangi Range in Ruahine Forest Park, McKinnon Hut is accessed via a long ridge climbing up from the Kawhatau Valley. Stay a night and return the same way.

trails.name: Mohi Bush Scenic Reserve walks
trails.description: Explore Mohi Bush and see if you can spot the tiny rifleman, New Zealand's smallest bird. This is one of the few places it is found outside of forest parks in Hawke's Bay.

trails.name: No Man's Road area tramping tracks
trails.description: Tracks starting at No Man's Road give access to a number of backcountry huts in the Eastern Ruahine Forest Park.

trails.name: Smiths Stream Hut track
trails.description: This tramping track takes you from the North Block Road carpark to Smiths Stream Hut.

trails.name: North Block Road walks
trails.description: North Block Road walking tracks include the walk to Triple X Hut and the Swamp Track.

trails.name: Otatara Pā Historic Reserve Walk
trails.description: This short, easy walk through Otatara Pā has archaeological features from one of the largest and most ancient Māori pā sites, as well as commanding views of the coastline - it's a good option for kids.

trails.name: Purity - Kelly Knight Hut Loop Track
trails.description: A loop track that takes in the Pourangaki River valley and the open tops of the Hikurangi Range in the Ruahine Forest Park.

trails.name: Purity Hut Track
trails.description: A steep climb to this hut nestled above the bushline on the Hikurangi Range in the Ruahine Forest Park.

trails.name: Purity to Kawhatau Base Track
trails.description: Walk along the tussock tops of the Hikurangi Range between Purity and McKinnon huts in the Ruahine Forest Park.

trails.name: Rangitane Road to Crow Hut Loop Track
trails.description: A very steep up and down tramp in Ruahine Forest Park, climbing first to the Mokai Patea Range, then dropping to the Kawhatau River and then back up to the Hikurangi Range and out to Rangitane Road via the Kawhatau River. Involves camping out.

trails.name: Rangiwahia Hut - Deadmans Track
trails.description: An easy to moderate overnight tramp to Rangiwahia Hut on the Whanahuia Range in Ruahine Forest Park. Return via the open tops and Deadman’s Track.

trails.name: Rangiwahia Hut Track
trails.description: This well-graded track to the tussock tops on Whanahuia Range is a great overnight tramp for families with children and new trampers.

trails.name: Renfrew Roadend to Table Flat Roadend
trails.description: Climb up and over the tussock tops of the Whanahuia Range in Ruahine Forest Park then drop down to follow the Oroua River valley.

trails.name: Ruahine Corner to Rangitane Road
trails.description: An excellent but strenuous trip in the remote north western Ruahine Forest Park renowned for its scenery and ecological values. Fly in by helicopter and walk out.

trails.name: Sunrise Track
trails.description: This well-graded track is a great overnight tramp for families with children and new trampers - it passes through changing forest types to the open tops, with great views of the Hawke’s Bay plains and excellent sunrises from the hut.

trails.name: Table Flat - Kashmir Roadend West-East crossing
trails.description: A west east traverse of the Ruahine Forest Park, following the Oroua River valley and over the main Ruahine Range to the Kashmir Roadend.

trails.name: Tangoio Falls Scenic Reserve tracks
trails.description: Discover Te Ana or Tangoio Falls on a short, easy walk suitable for kids, or take a longer walk through native bush on the Tangoio Walkway.

trails.name: Te Puia Hut Track  and Mangatainoka Hot Springs
trails.description: Walk to Te Puia Hut for an easy overnight tramp, or continue on to hot springs surrounded by bush and with river views.

trails.name: Wakarara Road tramping tracks
trails.description: Wakarara Road tramping tracks include tracks to Poutaki Hut, Parks Peak Hut and Sparrowhawk Bivouac and the Colenso Spur to the Main Range track.

trails.name: White Pine Bush Scenic Reserve walks
trails.description: Experience New Zealand's remaining coastal forest on these short, easy walks.

trails.name: Yeoman's Track
trails.description: This forest track follows an old route where logs were once hauled to Yeoman’s Mill. It is open to walkers and mountain bikers.

trails.name: Waipawa Forks Hut Track
trails.description: This track takes you from Sunrise Track to Waipawa Forks Hut.

trails.name: Longview Hut to Makaretu Hut Track
trails.description: A spectacular walk with panoramic views over the Hawke's Bay on a clear day.

trails.name: Tūtira Walkway
trails.description: Discover magnificent views of the Tūtira Station and the adjoining Lake Tūtira Recreation Reserve as you walk along the Tūtira Walkway.

trails.name: Waikopiro Loop Track
trails.description: A family-friendly, short walk around Lake Waikopiro that offers a chance to view native plants and wildlife.

trails.name: Ahuriri Estuary Track
trails.description:
View a community of wading birds, fish, mud dwellers and aquatic plant life from the estuary track and boardwalk. Over 70 species of resident and migratory waterbirds use the estuary as a feeding and resting area. Follow the track and boardwalk across the estuary.

The boardwalk stretches out across the water for good viewing. Take your binoculars.

trails.name: Tangoio Walkway
trails.description: Walking the track from north to south is recommended as the going is nearly all downhill. The track is steep in places and a stream crossing requires care after rain.

trails.name: Tutira Walkway
trails.description: Non-motorised boats only are permitted on the lake.

trails.name: Whakamaharatanga Walkway
trails.description: The walkway crosses farmland through two old pā sites with  terraces, pits and middens (shell dumps). Most of the farmland was under water prior to the Napier earthquake in 1931.

trails.name: Komata Hut Track
trails.description:
Follow the track as it ascends to the Komata high point (1083 m). At the junction near the high point, turn off onto the track signposted to Komata Hut (the main track continues on to Shutes Hut).
The track descends steeply into Ngaruroro Valley. Komata Hut is situated above Ngaruroro River, in a clearing.
